svg.text: Novak (2011) / Mayo Clinic notation · J Vis Exp 2011;53:e2502

text.tspan: HR min search interval = HR max + 30 s

svg.text: A = SBP drop at IIe = Baseline SBP − IIe nadir SBP
B = SBP recovery at IIl = max SBP(II late) − min SBP(PII late)
PRT = Pressure Recovery Time = time from SBP lowest in phase 3 until SBP returns to baseline
VR = Valsalva Ratio = max HR / min HR [vagal index]
HR min: lowest HR within 30 s of HR max (Novak 2011, Fig. 11; Freeman et al. 2022)
BRSa = Adrenergic baroreflex sensitivity = (A + B×0.75) / PRT [mmHg/s]
Refs: Novak P. J Vis Exp 2011;53:e2502 · Freeman R et al. Neurology 2005;65:1506 · Denq JC et al. J Clin Neurophysiol 1998;15:535
Baseline SBP: mean over 30 s window (−45 to −15 s before strain onset), avoiding anticipatory arousal artefact
